title: psilostrophe tagetina var. grandiflora
intro: Psilostrophe tagetina var. grandiflora, also known as the large-flowered paperflower or grandiflora paperflower, is a plant species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It is a perennial shrub with medicinal properties, traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The plant's roots, leaves, and flowers possess anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic properties, which contribute to its health benefits, such as reducing pain and inflammation, and promoting wound healing. Herbal preparations of Psilostrophe tagetina var. grandiflora can be made by infusing the plant's parts in water or oil, and it is also used in traditional medicine to create salves and tinctures.
